f63f2a26aa5e4703e36dbf17a8fce4239dc7aac2.tech: Mastra’s Observational Memory system for LLM agents uses two background agents—observer and reflector—to extract meaningful information while discarding noise. In the Mastra TypeScript agent framework, it applies prompt_engineering, agent_based and multi_agent_systems, plus token_optimization, semantic_search, latency_optimization, and cost_optimization. It also leverages langchain, fastapi, postgresql, and cache with prompt caching for cost efficiency.
f63f2a26aa5e4703e36dbf17a8fce4239dc7aac2.nontech: Mastra built an observational memory system for LLM agents that compresses conversations while keeping temporal awareness and contextual relevance. Using observer and reflector background agents, it retains what matters and lets details fade. The system was deployed in production across hiring and healthcare applications within the Mastra TypeScript agent framework, using prompt caching to improve cost efficiency and background compression to avoid blocking user interactions.

22e5eb503912eea7a819e8cbad7fb1d3e91f555f.tech: Shopify built a specialized agentic architecture for structured data extraction using DSPy and three independent React agents for fraud detection, merchant profiling, and tax categorization. The approach uses agent_based multi_agent_systems with prompt_engineering, plus evals, cost_optimization, and latency_optimization. It runs on kubernetes and leverages open_source and documentation.
22e5eb503912eea7a819e8cbad7fb1d3e91f555f.nontech: Shopify needed to extract structured information from millions of highly customized merchant storefronts, where lack of standardization made it hard to answer questions about products, brands, policies, or fraud indicators. It moved from a monolithic single-shot GPT-4/5 approach to a specialized multi-agent setup, improving quality metrics and reducing costs, enabling full coverage of all Shopify merchants rather than just 13%.
